patch.value: Add typed WebSocket session and exposure controls

Expose stable official Home Assistant WebSocket operations for panels, heartbeat, signed paths, and voice-assistant entity exposure through intuitive typed hassio commands while retaining raw ws call passthrough.

Typed commands cover get_panels, ping, auth/sign_path, and homeassistant/expose_entity list/update; Every command has detailed help and validates inputs before network mutation; All output formats remain schema-valid and source coverage stays 100/100/100/100; Live read-only operations and a reversible exposure update are verified against the configured Home Assistant instance
Official API contract and live instance behavior are captured; duplicate search finds no existing item; existing WebSocket architecture and tests are understood.
Agents can use security-sensitive stable WebSocket capabilities without hand-authoring protocol payloads.
Improves discoverability, validation, and automation safety for WebSocket workflows.
A documented, type-safe command family with live and automated proof.
The July parity work intentionally retained raw passthrough; current official docs and live 2026.4.4 behavior now identify the next stable high-value helpers.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Official Home Assistant REST, WebSocket, and authentication docs were refreshed on 2026-07-22; live raw calls confirm these four command families on Home Assistant 2026.4.4.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Never persist signed paths, tokens, entity inventories, coordinates, user IDs, or exposure payloads in pm evidence, repository files, logs, or PR text.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: The generic ws call surface provides forward compatibility, while stable documented operations benefit from typed validation and dedicated help.

patch.value: Delivered typed commands, bounded agent output, security guidance, complete documentation, and live verification.
All acceptance criteria pass without private data entering the repository or tracker.
Release verification, 100 percent coverage, installed CLI, six formats, live reads, reversible mutation, and secret scans passed.
Typed WebSocket session and exposure controls are production-ready
